name: MediaAlpha, Inc.
business: MediaAlpha provides a technology platform that enables insurance carriers and distributors to acquire consumers through a real-time, programmatic ecosystem. The company generates revenue by earning a fee for each consumer referral sold on its platform, with transactions based on qualifying actions like clicks, calls, or leads. As of 2025, the platform supported $2.2 billion in Transaction Value across property & casualty, health, and life insurance verticals.

exposures.sentence: Our technology platform brings together leading insurance carriers, agents, and high-intent consumers through a real-time, programmatic, transparent, and results-driven ecosystem.

tamClaims.claim: According to William Blair, advertising spend by P&C insurance carriers in the U.S. is expected to reach approximately $14 billion in 2026, growing at a 10% CAGR from 2024 levels.
tamClaims.sentence: According to William Blair, advertising spend by P&C insurance carriers in the U.S. is expected to reach approximately $14 billion in 2026, growing at a 10% CAGR from 2024 levels.

tamClaims.claim: Insurance companies wrote over $3 trillion in premiums in 2024, growing at a 10% CAGR from 2018, according to S&P Global Market Intelligence.
tamClaims.sentence: Insurance companies wrote over $3 trillion in premiums in 2024, growing at a 10% CAGR from 2018, according to S&P Global Market Intelligence.

tamClaims.claim: Total advertising spend in the P&C insurance industry was estimated to grow at 8% CAGR from 2018 to 2025, according to William Blair.
tamClaims.sentence: Total advertising spend in the P&C insurance industry was estimated to grow at 8% CAGR from 2018 to 2025, according to William Blair.

tamClaims.claim: According to eMarketer, companies across all industries allocated almost 80% of their advertising budgets to digital in 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: While eMarketer estimates that companies across all industries allocated almost 80% of their advertising budgets to digital in 2024, estimates based on public filings indicate that leading insurance companies collectively allocated less than 40% of their budgets to digital channels in that year.
